summary: The diff implements all three requirements. REQ-29 is addressed in App.tsx by querying the backend for an existing character after login and skipping CharacterCreationScreen when one is found. REQ-30 is addressed in backend/main.mo by adding a distinct #alreadyExists variant to CharacterCreationError and returning it from createCharacter. REQ-31 is addressed in CharacterCreation.tsx and App.tsx by catching CharacterAlreadyExistsError and silently routing to the character sheet. One minor concern: the backend createCharacter does not filter by realm when checking for an existing character (uses containsKey on principal only), which may not fully satisfy the per-realm aspect of the spec.

missing_requirements.requirement: If a character already exists for the selected realm in the current season, skip CharacterCreationScreen. The check must be realm-specific.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend getCharacter and createCharacter functions use a single Map keyed only by Principal (not by realm or season). This means there is no per-realm character lookup: a Hardcore character and a Softcore character cannot coexist for the same principal, and the frontend cannot distinguish which realm the existing character belongs to before routing. The spec requires the check and routing to be realm-specific.
